introSection: Titan is a Giovanola hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. Opened in 2001, the ride is almost identical to Goliath at Six Flags Magic Mountain, but it is elevated higher and has a 540 degree upward helix before the mid-course brakes.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The attraction was announced in November 2000. It opened on April 27, 2001.

The ride was repainted in 2025.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Titan is tall with a  drop at an angle of 65 degrees into a  long tunnel. A large turnaround follows, and then an airtime hill. Riders ascend the 540-degree upward helix before hitting the mid-course brake run. After a small drop and an overbanked turn, riders are thrown into the ride's 570-degree downward helix finale, which pulls 4.5 G's for nearly six seconds. A banked left turn follows, and then riders glide into the final brake run.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Blue track and white supports. Before 2025, the ride had orange track and blue supports.

fullText:
Titan is a Giovanola hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. Opened in 2001, the ride is almost identical to Goliath at Six Flags Magic Mountain, but it is elevated higher and has a 540 degree upward helix before the mid-course brakes.

The attraction was announced in November 2000. It opened on April 27, 2001.

The ride was repainted in 2025.

Titan is tall with a  drop at an angle of 65 degrees into a  long tunnel. A large turnaround follows, and then an airtime hill. Riders ascend the 540-degree upward helix before hitting the mid-course brake run. After a small drop and an overbanked turn, riders are thrown into the ride's 570-degree downward helix finale, which pulls 4.5 G's for nearly six seconds. A banked left turn follows, and then riders glide into the final brake run.

Blue track and white supports. Before 2025, the ride had orange track and blue supports.
